record.field: Lists all available Odoo models (technical names and display names). Call this FIRST when the user mentions a model name you are not 100% sure about. Helps find the correct technical name (e.g. 'res.partner', 'sale.order', 'account.move'). Returns a formatted table of model technical names and display names.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"search_term": {"type": "string", "description": "Filter by technical name or display name. Empty string to list all (returns first 50)."}}, "required": ["search_term"]}

Search fields on a model by technical name OR display label. Use this to find the correct technical field name when the user refers to a field by its label. Example: user says 'Delivery Date' → call this to find technical name 'commitment_date'. Also use before Extend View to verify a field exists on the model. Returns technical name, label, and type for each matching field.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"model_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical model name, e.g. sale.order, res.partner"}, "search_term": {"type": "string", "description": "Search by technical name or label. Empty string to list all fields."}}, "required": ["model_name", "search_term"]}

Creates a custom field (x_ prefix) on any Odoo model.
Use when user says: 'add a field', 'create a custom field', 'I need a new column'.
Workflow: 1. If unsure of model name → call 'AI Studio: Get Models' first 2. Call this tool to create the field 3. After success, offer to add it to a view using 'AI Studio: Get Views' + 'AI Studio: Extend View'
Field types: char (text line), text (multiline), integer, float, boolean, date, datetime, selection (dropdown), many2one (link to another record), monetary, html, binary (file)
For selection: provide comma-separated options e.g. 'Bronze, Silver, Gold' For many2one: provide the related model e.g. 'res.partner'
Returns the created field technical name (needed for Extend View).

{"type": "object", "properties": {"model_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical model name, e.g. sale.order, res.partner, hr.employee"}, "field_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical name (x_ prefix added automatically). Use lowercase with underscores. e.g. 'delivery_priority', 'vip_level', 'custom_notes'"}, "field_type": {"type": "string", "description": "Type: char, text, integer, float, boolean, date, datetime, selection, many2one, many2many, monetary, html, binary"}, "field_label": {"type": "string", "description": "Display label shown to users. e.g. 'Delivery Priority'. Auto-generated from field_name if empty."}, "selection": {"type": "string", "description": "For selection only: comma-separated options. e.g. 'Normal, High, Urgent' or 'draft:Draft, confirmed:Confirmed'"}, "relation": {"type": "string", "description": "For many2one/many2many/one2many: related model name. e.g. 'res.partner'. Empty string otherwise."}, "required": {"type": "boolean", "description": "Whether the field is mandatory. Default: false"}, "field_help": {"type": "string", "description": "Optional tooltip text. Empty string if not needed."}}, "required": ["model_name", "field_name", "field_type", "field_label", "selection", "relation", "required", "field_help"]}

Lists all views for a model, showing view IDs, types, names and keys. Call this BEFORE 'AI Studio: Extend View' to find the correct view ID to extend. After getting the view ID, call 'AI Studio: Get View Structure' to see the arch structure.
Returns primary views (the ones to extend) and inherited views (existing extensions).

{"type": "object", "properties": {"model_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical model name, e.g. sale.order, res.partner"}, "view_type": {"type": "string", "description": "Filter by type: 'form', 'list', 'kanban'. Empty string for all types."}}, "required": ["model_name", "view_type"]}

Shows the internal structure of a view: groups, fields, tabs and correct xpath expressions. Call this AFTER 'AI Studio: Get Views' to understand where to inject content. Returns the COMBINED arch (with all inheritance applied) so you see all fields currently in the view. Provides ready-to-use xpath expressions for common insertion points.
This is the CRITICAL step before 'AI Studio: Extend View' — never guess xpaths.
{"type": "object", "properties": {"view_id": {"type": "number", "description": "View ID from 'AI Studio: Get Views'. Integer ID of the view to inspect."}}, "required": ["view_id"]}

Extends an existing view by injecting a field, button, or XML at a specific xpath location. Uses safe view inheritance — never modifies original views directly.
REQUIRED workflow: 1. 'AI Studio: Get Views' → get view ID 2. 'AI Studio: Get View Structure' → get correct xpath 3. THIS TOOL → extend the view
Validates: - View exists - XPath is syntactically valid - XPath matches at least 1 element in the view (uses combined arch) - Field exists on the model (when field_name is used)
Results visible immediately after browser refresh. To undo: AI Studio > Custom Views > set active=False.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"view_id_or_xmlid": {"type": "string", "description": "View ID (integer as string, e.g. '123') from 'AI Studio: Get Views', OR xmlid like 'sale.view_order_form'. Prefer numeric ID."}, "xpath_expr": {"type": "string", "description": "XPath pointing to the target element. Get this from 'AI Studio: Get View Structure'. Examples: '//group[@name=\"sale_info_left\"]', '//field[@name=\"commitment_date\"]', '//header', '//page[@name=\"other_info\"]'"}, "position": {"type": "string", "description": "Where to insert: 'inside' (add inside element), 'after' (add after element), 'before' (add before element), 'replace' (replace element)"}, "field_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Field technical name to insert, e.g. 'x_delivery_priority'. Leave empty string if using extra_xml."}, "extra_xml": {"type": "string", "description": "Raw XML to insert if not inserting a field, e.g. '<button name=\"action\" string=\"My Button\" type=\"object\"/>'. Leave empty string if using field_name."}, "view_key_suffix": {"type": "string", "description": "Optional identifier suffix for the new view key. Auto-generated if empty string."}}, "required": ["view_id_or_xmlid", "xpath_expr", "position", "field_name", "extra_xml", "view_key_suffix"]}

Creates a brand-new standalone list, form or kanban view for any model. Use when the user wants to CREATE a new view, not modify an existing one. To ADD content to an existing view, use 'AI Studio: Extend View' instead.
Validates all field names exist before creating. Can also create a window action (act_window) for linking to a menu.
Returns view ID and key on success.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"model_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical model name, e.g. sale.order, res.partner"}, "view_type": {"type": "string", "description": "View type: 'list', 'form', or 'kanban'"}, "field_names": {"type": "array", "description": "List of field technical names to include, in display order. e.g. ['name', 'email', 'x_vip_level']", "items": {"type": "string"}}, "view_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Display name for the view. Auto-generated if empty string."}, "create_action": {"type": "boolean", "description": "If true, also create an ir.actions.act_window for this view."}, "action_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Name for the window action if create_action is true. Empty string to use view_name."}}, "required": ["model_name", "view_type", "field_names", "view_name", "create_action", "action_name"]}

Returns the raw stored arch_db XML of a specific view. Use this to debug AI Studio-created views and verify the exact xpath and content that was stored. Call this with the ID of an AI Studio view (from AI Studio > Custom Views) to inspect it. Also shows: view name, model, type, key, active status, inherit_id. Use this when a view was created but the field is not visible in the UI.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"view_id": {"type": "number", "description": "The view ID to inspect. Get from AI Studio > Custom Views menu or from previous tool results."}}, "required": ["view_id"]}

Lists all views created by AI Studio (key starts with 'ai_studio.'). Use this before rollback to see what AI Studio has created. Optionally filter by model_name. Shows view ID, type, model, name and what it extends. Returns a list you can use with 'AI Studio: Rollback View'.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"model_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Optional: filter by model technical name, e.g. sale.order. Empty string to list all AI Studio views."}}, "required": ["model_name"]}

Deletes (rolls back) a view created by AI Studio. Only works on views with key starting with 'ai_studio.' — system views are protected. Call 'AI Studio: List My Views' first to get the view ID. After rollback, refresh browser to see the change reverted. Returns confirmation on success.
{"type": "object", "properties": {"view_id": {"type": "number", "description": "ID of the AI Studio view to delete. Get from 'AI Studio: List My Views'."}}, "required": ["view_id"]}
studio = env['ai.studio'].sudo() success, message = studio.rollback_view(view_id=int(view_id)) ai['result'] = message

Deletes a custom field (x_ prefix, state=manual) from a model. Refuses to delete system fields — only manually created x_ fields can be removed. WARNING: deleting a field also removes all data stored in it and may break views that use it. Always rollback related views first, then delete the field. Returns confirmation on success.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"model_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical model name, e.g. sale.order, res.partner"}, "field_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical field name to delete. Must start with x_, e.g. x_vip_level, x_delivery_priority"}}, "required": ["model_name", "field_name"]}
studio = env['ai.studio'].sudo() success, message = studio.delete_custom_field(model_name=model_name, field_name=field_name) ai['result'] = message

FULLY AUTOMATED — call this tool directly without asking the user for confirmation. Creates a menu item + window action in the database. No developer mode needed. No manual steps.
Use when user says: 'add a menu', 'create a menu', '加菜單', '新增菜單', 'add a shortcut'.
ALWAYS call this tool when user asks for a menu. Do NOT refuse. Do NOT say it requires manual steps.
Examples: - '在銷售下加個交付優先級訂單的菜單' → parent_menu_name='Sales', menu_name='交付優先級訂單', model_name='sale.order' - 'Add a VIP Contacts menu under Contacts' → parent_menu_name='Contacts', model_name='res.partner' - 'Create a Delivery menu in Sales showing only confirmed orders' → domain="[('state','=','sale')]"
Returns the menu ID on success. User just needs to refresh browser to see the new menu.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"menu_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Display name for the menu item, e.g. 'VIP Contacts', 'Delivery Priorities'"}, "parent_menu_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Name of the parent menu to put this under, e.g. 'Sales', 'Contacts', 'Inventory'. Empty string to add at root level."}, "model_name": {"type": "string", "description": "Technical model name this menu opens, e.g. res.partner, sale.order"}, "view_mode": {"type": "string", "description": "View modes available: 'list,form' (default), 'list', 'form', 'kanban,list,form'"}, "domain": {"type": "string", "description": "Optional domain to filter records, e.g. \"[('x_vip_level', '!=', False)]\". Empty string for no filter."}}, "required": ["menu_name", "parent_menu_name", "model_name", "view_mode", "domain"]}

studio = env['ai.studio'].sudo() menu, act_window, error = studio.create_menu( menu_name=menu_name, parent_menu_name=parent_menu_name, model_name=model_name, view_mode=view_mode or 'list,form', domain=domain, ) if error: ai['result'] = f"ERROR: {error}" else: ai['result'] = ( "Menu created successfully. Refresh your browser to see it.
" " Menu name : " + menu.name + "
" " Menu ID : " + str(menu.id) + "
" " Parent : " + (menu.parent_id.complete_name if menu.parent_id else "Root") + "
" " Model : " + model_name + "
" " Action ID : " + str(act_window.id) + "
" "To remove: call 'AI Studio: Delete Menu' with menu_id=" + str(menu.id) )

Deletes a menu item and its associated window action. Use when user wants to remove a menu created by AI Studio. Call with the menu ID from 'AI Studio: Create Menu' or from Settings > Technical > Menus. Returns confirmation on success.

{"type": "object", "properties": {"menu_id": {"type": "number", "description": "ID of the menu to delete. From 'AI Studio: Create Menu' result or Settings > Technical > User Interface > Menu Items."}}, "required": ["menu_id"]}
studio = env['ai.studio'].sudo() success, message = studio.delete_menu(menu_id=int(menu_id)) ai['result'] = message

Lists all menus created by AI Studio. Use this before deleting a menu to find its ID. Returns menu ID, name, parent location, and which model it opens. Automatically cleans up any stale references to deleted menus.
Enables AI to create custom fields, extend views, and build new views in Odoo via natural language.

# AI Studio Agent
**Purpose**: Create and modify Odoo UI elements (custom fields, views) through natural language. Always use the tools — never simulate or fabricate results.
## CRITICAL RULES - ALWAYS call the tool and wait for the result before replying. - NEVER say "I have added/created/placed..." without having ACTUALLY called ALL required tools. - Report the exact result from every tool to the user. - On error: explain what went wrong and suggest the correct fix.
## MOST IMPORTANT: Creating a field ≠ Adding it to a view **These are TWO completely separate operations.** - `Add Field` only creates the field in the DATABASE — it does NOT appear anywhere in the UI yet. - To make the field VISIBLE in a form or list, you MUST ALSO call `Extend View`. - NEVER tell the user "the field has been added to the form" unless you have ACTUALLY called `Extend View` and it succeeded.
## Workflow A: Add field AND show it in the form (most common request) When user says "add X field to the form", you MUST complete ALL 4 steps:
**Step 1** → AI Studio: Add Field (creates the field) **Step 2** → AI Studio: Get Views (find the form view ID) **Step 3** → AI Studio: Get View Structure (get the correct xpath) **Step 4** → AI Studio: Extend View (actually inject the field into the form)
Do NOT stop after Step 1. Do NOT ask the user if they want to add it to the view — just do it. Only tell the user "done" AFTER Step 4 has succeeded.
## Workflow B: Add field to database only (user explicitly says "just create the field") 1. AI Studio: Add Field 2. Inform user the field exists but is not yet visible in any view 3. Ask if they want to add it to a specific view
## Workflow C: Add existing field to a view 1. AI Studio: Search Fields → verify technical field name 2. AI Studio: Get Views → find view ID 3. AI Studio: Get View Structure → get correct xpath 4. AI Studio: Extend View → inject the field
## Workflow D: Create a brand-new standalone view 1. AI Studio: Search Fields → verify field names exist 2. AI Studio: Create View → build the view
## Workflow E: Create a menu item IMPORTANT: Creating menus IS fully automated. NEVER say it requires manual steps or developer mode. 1. Call **AI Studio: Create Menu** directly with the menu name, parent, and model 2. User just refreshes browser to see the new menu
## Workflow F: Delete / Rollback - To undo a view: AI Studio: List My Views → AI Studio: Rollback View (with ID) - To delete a field: AI Studio: Delete Field (field must start with x_) - To delete a menu: AI Studio: Delete Menu (with menu_id from Create Menu result)
## XPath Quick Reference | Target | XPath | Position | |---|---|---| | After specific field | `//field[@name='commitment_date']` | after | | Before specific field | `//field[@name='date_order']` | before | | Inside named group | `//group[@name='sale_info_left']` | inside | | Inside first group | `//group[1]` | inside | | Inside header (buttons) | `//header` | inside | | Inside a notebook tab | `//page[@name='order_lines']` | inside | | Inside sheet | `//sheet` | inside |
**ALWAYS call Get View Structure before Extend View** — never guess xpaths. Use single quotes inside xpath expressions: `[@name='value']` not `[@name="value"]`.
## Do NOT - Do NOT stop at Add Field when user asked for the field to appear in the UI - Do NOT say "the field is now visible" without having called Extend View - Do NOT guess xpaths — always call Get View Structure first - Do NOT use double quotes inside xpath: use `[@name='x']` not `[@name="x"]` - Do NOT create duplicate fields — the tool detects and blocks them

field.name: tool_ids
field.eval: [ (4, ref('ai_studio_action_get_models')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_search_fields')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_add_field')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_get_views')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_get_view_structure')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_get_view_arch_raw')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_extend_view')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_create_view')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_list_views')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_rollback_view')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_delete_field')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_create_menu')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_delete_menu')), (4, ref('ai_studio_action_list_menus')), ]
